main_features.description: Maintained by over 1,600 contributors on GitHub to ensure the information on free tier offerings remains current and accurate.
Organizes hundreds of services into specialized sections such as Cloud Providers, Managed Databases, CI/CD, CMS, and Generative AI.
Only lists services with actual free tiers (not trials) that last at least a year and meet specific security requirements like TLS support.
Specifically targets the needs of infrastructure developers, system administrators, and DevOps practitioners.

og.title: Flowbite React - UI Component Library
og.description: Flowbite React is an open-source UI component library built with React components, Tailwind CSS utility classes and based on the Flowbite design system and components.

name: Flowbite React
description: An open-source UI component library for React applications built with Tailwind CSS utility classes and based on the Flowbite design system.

main_features.description: A collection of over 100+ interactive components including navbars, modals, dropdowns, and carousels.
Built natively with Tailwind CSS utility classes, allowing for seamless styling and rapid customization.
Fully typed components providing a robust developer experience with autocompletion and type safety.
Built-in configuration for dark mode, allowing users to toggle between light and dark themes easily.
Offers a flexible theme object to customize the design system at both global and component levels.

og.title: Zustand
og.description: 🐻 Bear necessities for state management in React

name: Zustand
description: A small, fast, and scalable bearbones state-management solution for React that uses simplified flux principles and hooks.

main_features.description: Reduces code complexity by eliminating the need for providers, actions, and reducers to manage global state.
Uses React hooks as the primary method for consuming and updating state, making it intuitive for modern React developers.
Provides fine-grained control over re-renders by allowing components to subscribe only to specific slices of the state.
Includes built-in support for middleware such as persistence, logging, and integration with Redux DevTools.
While primarily used with React, its core logic can be used in any JavaScript environment without dependency on React's lifecycle.

og.title: XState Visualizer
og.description: Visualizer for XState state machines and statecharts

name: XState Visualizer
description: An interactive tool for visualizing, simulating, and debugging XState state machines and statecharts to better understand and communicate complex application logic.

main_features.description: Automatically generates interactive statechart diagrams from XState machine code in real-time.
Allows users to trigger events and transitions manually to test the logic and flow of their state machines.
Features a built-in editor with TypeScript support for writing and refining machine definitions directly in the browser.
Enables users to save their visualizations and share them via unique URLs for documentation or team reviews.
Provides a detailed view of the current state, context, and transition history during simulation.

og.title: Jotai, primitive and flexible state management for React
og.description: Jotai takes a bottom-up approach to global React state management with an atomic model inspired by Recoil. One can build state by combining atoms and renders are optimized based on atom dependency. This solves the extra re-render issue of React context and eliminates the need for memoization.

name: Jotai
description: A primitive and flexible state management library for React that uses a bottom-up atomic approach to optimize re-renders and eliminate manual memoization.

main_features.description: Build state by combining small, independent units called atoms, inspired by Recoil's bottom-up approach.
Automatically optimizes renders based on atom dependency, solving the common over-rendering issues found in React Context.
Eliminates the need for manual memoization (like useMemo) as the library handles dependency tracking internally.
Offers a simple and familiar API that feels like using React's built-in useState hook but for global state.

og.title: Valtio, makes proxy-state simple for React and Vanilla

name: Valtio
description: A lightweight proxy-based state management library for React and Vanilla JavaScript that simplifies reactive state handling through direct object mutation.

main_features.description: Uses JavaScript Proxies to track changes to state objects, enabling automatic updates without manual boilerplate or complex reducers.
Allows developers to modify state by directly mutating properties, which Valtio automatically converts into immutable snapshots for React.
The useSnapshot hook tracks property access and only triggers re-renders when the specific parts of the state being used actually change.
Works seamlessly outside of React environments, making it a versatile choice for any JavaScript application or utility.
Provides built-in support for Redux DevTools, allowing for easy debugging, state inspection, and time-traveling.

og.title: HeroUI (Previously NextUI) - Beautiful, fast and modern React UI Library

og.description: A collection of modern, server-safe React hooks – from the ui.dev team
og.title: useHooks – The React Hooks Library

main_features.description: All hooks are designed to be safe for Server-Side Rendering (SSR), making them compatible with frameworks like Next.js.
Built with TypeScript to provide strong typing, autocompletion, and compile-time error checking for a better developer experience.
Includes a wide range of hooks for common tasks, such as useLocalStorage, useWindowSize, useDebounce, and useIntersectionObserver.
Hooks are designed to be imported individually, ensuring that your final bundle remains lean and tree-shakable.

og.title: Next.js video embedding with next-video

main_features.description: Automatically compresses and converts video files into web-optimized formats for high-performance delivery via CDN.
Moves large video files out of your Git repository to external providers like Mux, Vercel Blob, or S3 to prevent slow builds and storage issues.
Implements HLS/DASH adaptive bitrate streaming to ensure smooth playback and prevent buffering on slow network connections.

og.title: luxon - Immutable date wrapper
og.description: Immutable date wrapper

name: Luxon
description: A powerful, modern, and friendly wrapper for JavaScript dates and times, featuring an immutable, chainable API with native time zone and internationalization support.

main_features.description: All Luxon objects are immutable, meaning methods return new instances rather than modifying the original object, leading to fewer bugs.
Built-in support for IANA time zones and offsets using the browser's native capabilities, eliminating the need for large external data files.
Uses the native Intl API for localized formatting and parsing, ensuring high performance and a smaller bundle size.
Provides dedicated classes for handling durations (e.g., '3 days') and intervals (e.g., 'from Monday to Wednesday') with precise math.
Offers a clean, modern, and explicit API that allows for easy chaining of parsing, manipulation, and formatting operations.

og.title: Home | React Haiku

description: A minimal and lightweight React Hooks library that provides a comprehensive collection of hooks and utilities to simplify development and reduce boilerplate code.

main_features.description: Provides over 39 custom hooks covering use cases like battery status, clipboard management, debouncing, and geolocation.
Includes specialized components like <For>, <If>, and <Show> to simplify conditional and list rendering in JSX.
Features a small bundle size (approx. 6KB) and supports tree-shaking to ensure only used code is included in production.
Built entirely with TypeScript, offering comprehensive type definitions and enhanced developer experience through auto-completion.
Maintains a clean dependency tree by operating without third-party packages, reducing potential security risks and conflicts.

Enables complex parallax animations easily through simple data attributes like data-scroll-speed without writing custom math.
Exposes real-time scroll progress as CSS variables or JavaScript events, perfect for creating scroll-driven UI interactions.
Automatically optimizes performance on mobile devices by disabling parallax effects while maintaining native touch scrolling.

Usually this takes about one week for us to get your requirements and return a fully rendered illustration but it could take longer depending on how booked we are.To purchase a custom illustration select "Custom Illustration" and enter your email. We will contact you within 24 hours to start the process.The artists behind HumanitiesHumanities are drawn by Levi and Lily Nunnink in Tennessee. og.title: Grid Garden
og.description: A game for learning CSS grid layout

og.title: Learn Git Branching

description: An interactive Git visualization tool and tutorial that uses gamified challenges to help users master branching, merging, and rebasing through a real-time commit tree.

main_features.description: Provides a real-time, animated graphical representation of the Git repository tree as commands are entered.
Features a structured curriculum covering everything from basic commits to advanced concepts like cherry-picking and interactive rebasing.

Allows users to build custom Git challenges and share them via URLs to help others learn specific workflows.
The interface and tutorials are translated into dozens of languages, making it accessible to a global developer community.

og.title: SQL Murder Mystery
og.description: Use SQL queries to solve the murder mystery. Suitable for beginners or experienced SQL sleuths.

name: SQL Murder Mystery
description: An interactive educational game designed to teach SQL concepts through a self-directed murder mystery investigation in the fictional SQL City.

main_features.description: Execute real SQLite queries directly in the browser to query crime scene reports, witness statements, and personal records.
A guided tutorial that introduces essential SQL commands like SELECT, WHERE, and JOIN for those new to database querying.
Provides a detailed Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) to help users understand the structure and connections between the nine database tables.
Experienced users can bypass the tutorial and use advanced SQL techniques to solve the case in any way they choose.
A dedicated solution table allows users to verify their findings by inserting the suspect's name to receive immediate feedback.

og.title: ApexCharts.js - Open Source JavaScript Charts for your website
og.description: ApexCharts is a a free and open-source modern charting library that helps developers to create beautiful and interactive visualizations for web pages.

name: ApexCharts
description: A modern, open-source JavaScript charting library that helps developers build interactive, responsive, and beautiful data visualizations with a simple API.

main_features.description: Built-in support for zooming, panning, scrolling, and informative tooltips to help users explore data effectively.
Offers official wrappers for popular modern frameworks including React, Vue, and Angular, as well as vanilla JavaScript.
Supports a wide range of visualizations such as line, area, bar, pie, donut, heatmap, treemap, and candlestick charts.
Charts are fully responsive and scale gracefully across desktops, tablets, and mobile devices with customizable breakpoints.
Allows for real-time data updates and smooth animations when changing datasets or interacting with the charts.

og.description: A framework for mostly-reusable graphics with svelte
og.title: Layer Cake

name: Layer Cake
description: A headless graphics framework for Svelte that provides a shared coordinate system and scales for creating responsive, multi-layered data visualizations.

main_features.description: Provides the essential logic, scales, and coordinate systems without forcing specific chart types, allowing for complete design customization.
Allows developers to stack and sync SVG, HTML, Canvas, and WebGL layers within the same shared coordinate space.
Automatically measures the target container and data extents to keep scales perfectly synced during layout changes and screen resizing.
Supports generating responsive graphics on the server that can function without client-side JavaScript.
Encourages building a library of modular, reusable chart layers like axes and annotations that live directly within your Svelte project.

og.title: GitHub - atinux/npm-chart: Visualize npm downloads in a beautiful chart, ready to be shared with your community.
og.description: Visualize npm downloads in a beautiful chart, ready to be shared with your community. - atinux/npm-chart

main_features.description: Visualize and compare download trends for multiple npm packages simultaneously on a single interactive line chart.
Generate and download high-quality images of package statistics for use in READMEs, documentation, or presentations.
Built-in support for generating beautiful Open Graph images optimized for sharing package growth on social media platforms.
Runs on the edge using NuxtHub with aggressive caching to provide near-instant loading of package metrics.
Fetches the latest download statistics directly from the npm registry via the npm-stat.com API.

og.title: Plotteus | The JavaScript library for data storytelling

description: An open-source JavaScript library specifically designed for data storytelling, enabling seamless transitions and full animation control for interactive data-driven narratives.

main_features.description: Smoothly morphs between different chart types and data elements, including colors and positions, to maintain context during storytelling.
Provides granular control over animations, allowing readers to progress through data stories at their own pace via scroll or step triggers.
Uses a declarative configuration approach where stories are defined by an array of steps, making complex visualizations easier to manage.
Includes built-in support for responsive design and adaptable themes that can match any background color or brand identity.
Supports multiple visualization types including bar charts, bubble charts, scatter plots, and treemaps within a single narrative flow.

description: A comprehensive collection of hundreds of charts made with React and D3.js, featuring step-by-step explanations and reproducible code snippets for every visualization.

Teaches a unique approach using D3.js for mathematical calculations and React for DOM rendering.
Covers a wide range of visualizations from basic bar charts to complex maps, networks, and circular packing.
Includes detailed explanations on building components from scratch, including axes, scales, and responsiveness.

description: A composable and reliable charting library built on React components, designed to help developers create interactive data visualizations with ease using a declarative API.

main_features.description: Quickly build complex charts by combining decoupled and reusable React components like Line, Bar, and XAxis.
Built on top of SVG elements with a lightweight dependency on D3 submodules for crisp and scalable visuals.
Simplifies chart creation by allowing developers to define chart structures and data mappings using standard React props.
Includes a ResponsiveContainer component that ensures charts automatically adapt to the size of their parent container.
Offers extensive control over chart appearance through props, custom tooltips, and the ability to pass in custom SVG elements.

og.title: Svender Charts - A Svelte Component Charting Library
og.description: Interactive Plug and Play chart components for your Svelte app

main_features.description: Modular chart components that can be easily integrated into Svelte projects via simple copy-paste or standard imports.
Features a real-time editor with sliders and toggles to adjust chart properties like margins, colors, and tick counts instantly.
Harnesses the flexibility of D3.js for high-quality visualizations while abstracting complex imperative code into declarative Svelte syntax.
Includes detailed data schemas for every chart type to ensure data is correctly formatted before implementation.

og.title: Overview · Cloudflare D1 docs
og.description: D1 is Cloudflare's managed, serverless database with SQLite's SQL semantics, built-in disaster recovery, and Worker and HTTP API access.

name: Cloudflare D1
description: D1 is Cloudflare's managed, serverless relational database built on SQLite, designed to integrate natively with Cloudflare Workers and Pages.

main_features.description: Provides full SQLite SQL semantics in a managed environment without the need for manual server provisioning or maintenance.
Built-in disaster recovery that allows you to restore your database to any specific minute within the last 30 days.
Supports multi-tenant architectures by allowing developers to create thousands of independent databases at no extra cost for isolation.
Automatically distributes data replicas across Cloudflare's global network to minimize latency for read-heavy applications.
Native bindings for Cloudflare Workers and Pages, with support for popular ORMs like Drizzle and access via an HTTP API.

og.title: dbdiagram.io - Database Relationship Diagrams Design Tool
og.description: Quick and simple free tool to help you draw your database relationship diagrams and flow quickly using simple DSL language.

name: dbdiagram.io
description: A web-based tool for designing database relationship diagrams (ERDs) using a simple DSL language called DBML.

main_features.description: Design database schemas using a keyboard-centric, simple markup language (DBML) that instantly updates the visual diagram.
Import existing SQL scripts from PostgreSQL, MySQL, or SQL Server and export diagrams to SQL, PDF, or image formats.
Share diagrams with team members to collaborate in real-time and manage project versions with history support.
Generate and modify database schemas by describing requirements in natural language through the built-in AI assistant.
Group tables, customize colors, and use auto-layout features to maintain clarity in complex database architectures.

og.title: Dataflare - Fast, Simple Database Manager
og.description: Fast, simple, cross-platform database manager with intuitive GUI to connect, query, and manage 20+ databases: PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, ClickHouse, DuckDB, Redis, S3/R2, and more.

name: Dataflare
description: A fast, cross-platform database manager with an intuitive GUI to connect, query, and manage over 20 database types including SQL, NoSQL, and cloud storage.

main_features.description: Unified interface to connect with 20+ databases including PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, ClickHouse, DuckDB, Redis, and S3/R2 buckets.
Utilize AI to generate SQL queries, explain complex code snippets, and receive intelligent suggestions for faster development.
Quickly populate tables with hundreds of thousands of rows of mock data to facilitate robust testing and performance benchmarking.
Enhanced security feature that prevents accidental data changes by allowing users to browse and query in a protected state.
Easily view table relationships through a schema manager and analyze data sets using built-in visualization charts.

og.description: Amazon DynamoDB is a fully managed, serverless, key-value NoSQL database that runs high-performance applications at any scale, with built-in security, continuous backups, and automated multi-region replication.
og.title: Amazon DynamoDB

name: Amazon DynamoDB
description: A fully managed, serverless, key-value NoSQL database service designed to run high-performance applications at any scale with built-in security and continuous backups.

main_features.description: Eliminates the need to provision or manage servers, automatically scaling capacity to meet workload demands.
Provides fully managed, multi-region, multi-active replication for globally distributed applications with high availability.
Delivers consistent, single-digit millisecond response times for read and write operations at any scale.
Includes encryption at rest by default and integrates with AWS IAM for fine-grained access control and identity management.
Offers point-in-time recovery and on-demand backups to protect data against accidental deletion or corruption.

name: ElectricSQL
description: An open-source sync layer for Postgres that enables building local-first, real-time applications with bi-directional active replication and offline support.

main_features.description: Provides bi-directional synchronization between server-side Postgres and local client-side databases like SQLite or PGlite.
Allows developers to sync specific subsets of data to clients using a flexible Shape-based filtering API to optimize performance.
Employs CRDT-based logic and causal consistency to handle concurrent edits and maintain referential integrity across all nodes.
Enables sub-second data propagation and instant reactivity for high-performance collaborative and multi-user applications.
Automatically generates type-safe database clients to ensure data consistency and developer productivity between the backend and frontend.

og.title: MySQL Visual Explain
og.description: Optimize MySQL queries with easy-to-understand Visual Explains.

name: MySQL Visual Explain
description: A web-based tool that transforms cryptic MySQL EXPLAIN output into easy-to-understand visual execution plans to help developers optimize slow database queries.

main_features.description: Converts complex text-based EXPLAIN output into an intuitive graphical flow showing how MySQL processes a query.
Automatically highlights performance issues like full table scans, filesort operations, and temporary table creation.
Compatible with various MySQL output formats including TRADITIONAL, JSON, and the modern TREE format used in MySQL 8.0+.
Provides an interactive interface to drill down into specific join types, cost estimates, and row counts for each execution step.
Offers a free public API allowing developers to integrate visual explain capabilities directly into their own internal monitoring tools.

og.title: PGlite
og.description: Lightweight WASM Postgres

name: PGlite
description: A lightweight WebAssembly (WASM) build of PostgreSQL that allows running a full Postgres database directly in the browser, Node.js, Bun, and Deno with no external dependencies.

main_features.description: Runs a full PostgreSQL engine compiled to WebAssembly for high performance in JavaScript and TypeScript environments.
Supports saving data to IndexedDB in browsers or the local file system in server-side runtimes like Node.js and Bun.
Includes support for popular PostgreSQL extensions such as pgvector for vector search and RAG applications.
Features a live query API that allows developers to subscribe to data changes and receive real-time updates.
Packages the entire database into a single ~3MB gzipped library with no need for a separate database server process.

og.title: PlanetScale - the world’s fastest and most scalable cloud hosting for Vitess and Postgres
og.description: PlanetScale offers the world’s fastest and most scalable cloud hosting for Vitess and Postgres.

name: PlanetScale
description: A serverless relational database platform built on Vitess and Postgres that offers horizontal scaling, database branching, and zero-downtime schema changes.

main_features.description: Git-like branching workflow that allows developers to create isolated environments for testing schema changes before deploying to production.
Deploy schema migrations without locking tables or causing application downtime, ensuring continuous availability during updates.
Built-in support for transparent horizontal sharding via Vitess, enabling databases to scale across multiple nodes to handle massive workloads.
An integrated query performance monitoring tool that provides real-time observability and actionable recommendations to optimize database performance.
High-performance database instances running on locally-attached NVMe storage for maximum IOPS and ultra-low latency.

og.title: Prisma | Instant Postgres plus an ORM for simpler db workflows
og.description: Build, fortify, and grow your application easily with an intuitive data model, type-safety, automated migrations, connection pooling and caching.

name: Prisma
description: An open-source next-generation ORM for Node.js and TypeScript that simplifies database access, migrations, and management with a type-safe API.

main_features.description: An auto-generated, type-safe, and intuitive query builder for Node.js and TypeScript that prevents runtime errors.
A declarative data modeling and migration tool that automatically handles database schema changes and history.
A visual editor and GUI for your database that allows you to explore, filter, and manipulate data without writing SQL.
A global database cache and high-performance connection pooler designed to speed up queries in serverless and edge environments.
A managed service that enables real-time database events, allowing applications to react to data changes instantly.

og.title: Redis - The Real-time Data Platform
og.description: Developers love Redis. Unlock the full potential of the Redis database with Redis Enterprise and start building blazing fast apps.

name: Redis
description: An open-source, in-memory data structure store used as a distributed key-value database, cache, and message broker.

main_features.description: Provides sub-millisecond response times by storing data in RAM, making it ideal for real-time applications.
Supports diverse data types including strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets, bitmaps, and geospatial indexes.
Utilizes Redis Sentinel for automatic failover and Redis Cluster for horizontal scaling across multiple nodes.
Offers RDB (point-in-time snapshots) and AOF (append-only logs) to ensure data durability on disk.
Built-in support for publish/subscribe messaging patterns for high-performance communication between applications.

og.title: SQLocal
og.description: SQLocal makes it easy to run SQLite3 in the browser, backed by the origin private file system.

name: SQLocal
description: A library for running SQLite3 in the browser with high-performance persistence via the Origin Private File System (OPFS).

main_features.description: Executes any standard SQLite query directly in the browser using the official WebAssembly build of SQLite3.
Runs the SQLite engine in a dedicated web worker to ensure database operations do not block the main UI thread.
Leverages the Origin Private File System for fast, reliable, and persistent file-based storage on the user's device.
Provides specialized drivers and hooks for Kysely and Drizzle ORM to enable type-safe database queries.
Allows developers to subscribe to query results and receive real-time updates even across multiple browser tabs.

og.title: Sequelize
og.description: Sequelize is a modern TypeScript and Node.js ORM for Oracle, Postgres, MySQL, MariaDB, SQLite and SQL Server, and more. Featuring solid transaction support, relations, eager and lazy loading, read replication and more.

name: Sequelize
description: A modern TypeScript and Node.js ORM for various SQL databases, featuring solid transaction support, relations, and advanced loading techniques.

main_features.description: Supports a wide range of databases including PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, SQLite, MS SQL Server, Oracle, and Snowflake.
Provides first-class TypeScript support with decorators and type safety for modern application development.
Simplifies complex database relationships with built-in support for One-to-One, One-to-Many, and Many-to-Many associations.
Offers robust support for ACID transactions, allowing developers to ensure data integrity across multiple operations.
Optimizes data retrieval by allowing developers to choose between loading associated models immediately or on-demand.

og.title: TablePlus | Modern, Native Tool for Database Management.
og.description: Modern, native client with intuitive GUI tools to create, access, query & edit multiple relational databases: MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, Microsoft SQL Server, Amazon Redshift, MariaDB, CockroachDB, Vertica, Cassandra, and Redis.

name: TablePlus
description: A modern, native database management tool with an intuitive GUI to create, access, query, and edit multiple relational and NoSQL databases.

main_features.description: Built natively for macOS, Windows, and Linux to ensure a fast, lightweight, and stable user experience.
Simultaneously manage multiple databases including MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, SQL Server, Redis, and Cassandra.
Features a smart query editor with instant autocomplete, syntax highlighting, split panes, and query history.
Enables inline editing of data rows and table structures with advanced filters for efficient record lookups.
Provides built-in SSH tunneling and TLS encryption to ensure end-to-end security for remote database access.

og.title: Tembo — Delegate work to any coding agent
og.description: Tembo works autonomously with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and more to ship features, review code, and take on work from Slack, Linear, or anywhere else.

name: Tembo

main_features.description: Enables developers to delegate work to various AI agents including Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex, with the ability to swap agents at the task level.
Deeply integrates with Slack, Linear, GitHub, and Jira, allowing users to assign issues to AI agents using mentions or status changes.
Monitors production error tools like Sentry to automatically diagnose issues, generate code fixes, and submit pull requests.
Runs agent-generated code in secure, isolated environments to verify solutions before they are submitted for human review.
Supports scheduled tasks for maintaining documentation, refactoring technical debt, and generating changelogs based on repository changes.

og.title: Turso - Databases Everywhere
og.description: Turso is the lightweight database that scales to millions of agents. Deploy databases everywhere—on servers, in browsers, on devices—just like files. A full SQLite drop-in replacement, built for the agentic future.

name: Turso
description: A lightweight, edge-compatible database built on libSQL that scales to millions of instances, offering native vector search and global replication for modern applications.

main_features.description: Provides native similarity search for AI applications and RAG workflows without the need for additional extensions.
Allows for global data distribution with low-latency access by placing data closer to users and edge compute.
Supports spinning up thousands of isolated databases, making it ideal for per-user data isolation and SaaS applications.
Functions as a full drop-in replacement for SQLite, maintaining familiar syntax while adding cloud-native capabilities.
Enables the creation of isolated copy-on-write branches for testing and development environments.

og.title: Upstash: Serverless Data Platform
og.description: Upstash is a serverless data platform providing low latency and high scalability for real-time applications. Optimize your data infrastructure with Upstash's managed services for Redis, Vector, QStash, and other key data technologies.

name: Upstash
description: A serverless data platform providing low-latency, auto-scaling managed services for Redis, Vector databases, and messaging via QStash.

main_features.description: Fully managed Redis with per-request pricing, automatic scaling, and multi-region replication for global low latency.
A serverless vector store designed for AI applications, supporting high-speed embedding storage and semantic search.
An HTTP-based messaging and scheduling service for background jobs and reliable task delivery in serverless environments.
Native support for HTTP requests, allowing developers to connect to databases from edge functions and serverless workers without persistent connections.
Active-active replication across multiple regions to ensure data availability and high performance for global users.

og.title: Code Hike
og.description: Use Markdown and React to build rich content websites. Documentation, tutorials, blogs, videos, interactive walkthroughs, and more.

og.description: The cross-platform app building toolkit

description: An open-source framework for building tiny, fast, and secure cross-platform desktop and mobile applications using web technologies and a Rust backend.

main_features.description: Build applications for Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android from a single codebase using web technologies.
Leverages native OS webview renderers to produce extremely lightweight binaries, with some apps starting as small as 600KB.
Provides a hardened isolation pattern and a granular, ACL-based permission system to minimize the application's attack surface.
Compatible with any frontend framework that compiles to HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, including React, Vue, Svelte, and Solid.
Offers a powerful inter-process communication (IPC) bridge to access native system APIs via Rust, Swift, or Kotlin.

og.title: Unovis

Allows developers to import only the specific components they need, significantly reducing the final application bundle size.
Includes a wide variety of visualizations such as XY-charts, maps, network graphs, Sankey diagrams, and sunbursts.
Provides highly customizable styling through CSS variables, making it easy to match any design system or brand identity.
Built entirely with TypeScript to provide robust type safety and a superior developer experience.

main_features.description: Encodes images into extremely short ASCII strings (typically 20-30 characters) that are small enough to be stored in databases or included directly in JSON responses.
The hash is decoded on the client side into a small, blurred image that scales to any size, eliminating the need for additional network requests for placeholder images.
Provides official and community-driven implementations for a wide range of languages and platforms, including Swift, Kotlin, TypeScript, C, Python, and Rust.
Replaces jarring blank spaces or generic loading boxes with a colorful, blurred preview that matches the original image's color palette and composition.

og.title: GitHub - ts-safeql/safeql: Validate and auto-generate TypeScript types from raw SQL queries in PostgreSQL.

main_features.description: Automatically checks raw SQL queries for syntax errors and schema mismatches, such as misspelled columns or tables, during linting.

og.description: Amazon S3 is cloud object storage with industry-leading scalability, data availability, security, and performance. S3 is ideal for data lakes, mobile applications, backup and restore, archival, IoT devices, ML, AI, and analytics.

name: LangGraph
description: A framework for building stateful, multi-agent applications with LLMs by modeling agent workflows as directed acyclic or cyclic graphs.

main_features.description: Enables loops and iterations within agentic workflows, which are essential for self-correction and multi-step reasoning.

Provides first-class support for manual approvals or human interventions to guide agent decision-making.
Simplifies the orchestration of multiple specialized agents working together to solve complex, distributed problems.
Allows developers to inspect, rewind, and replay agent states to identify and fix logic errors in complex graphs.
